title: Partial Clone, Sparse Checkout, and Large-Repository Scale · Graph Lens: Gita Scale Fallback Transfer
summary: A multi-gigabyte monorepo has slow clone and status operations, developers need different cones, CI requires complete release objects, and an unreliable promisor remote may disappear. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita scale fallback transfer increment in large-repository data and working-set contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify required commands and tools against changed working sets, define full-object release gates, preserve an offline fallback, and document experimental limits.
Measure clone transfer, object count, index size, status latency, checkout, fetch, build input, cache, and developer task before choosing an optimization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Scale Fallback Transfer
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G88 is a theory in a disposable repository. the failing build omits a required shared package from the declared cone. The learner must predict the active sparse paths expose the intended working set without claiming object completeness, run git sparse-checkout list, explain apps/status is present while packages/shared is absent from the build working set, test this changed condition: add packages/shared and repeat both sparse-index status and full-object release verification, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the prior sparse specification and support disabling sparse checkout reversibly.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Scale Fallback Transfer contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify required commands and tools against changed working sets, define full-object release gates, preserve an offline fallback, and document experimental limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G88 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the failing build omits a required shared package from the declared cone. The learner must predict the active sparse paths expose the intended working set without claiming object completeness, run git sparse-checkout list, explain apps/status is present while packages/shared is absent from the build working set, test this changed condition: add packages/shared and repeat both sparse-index status and full-object release verification, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the prior sparse specification and support disabling sparse checkout reversibly. Competency probe: Verify required commands and tools against changed working sets, define full-object release gates, preserve an offline fallback, and document experimental limits. A defensible solution applies Gita Scale Fallback Transfer, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to large-repository data and working-set contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-scale-fallback-transfer — Verify required commands and tools against changed working sets, define full-object release gates, preserve an offline fallback, and document experimental limits.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the failing build omits a required shared package from the declared cone
git sparse-checkout list
# expected: the active sparse paths expose the intended working set without claiming object completeness
# observation: apps/status is present while packages/shared is absent from the build working set
# changed-case: add packages/shared and repeat both sparse-index status and full-object release verification
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain the prior sparse specification and support disabling sparse checkout reversibly
# transfer-limit: full release and offline recovery need explicit promised-object and promisor-availability checks
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Scale Fallback Transfer evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-scale-fallback-transfer-nnxyqh" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Scale Fallback Transfer into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-scale-fallback-transfer-nnxyqh
# scenario-key: trace-nnxyqh probe-hqyxnn boundary-xyqhnn owner-nnxyqhx fault-nnxyqhz
# before-ref: main=a11cenn candidate=b00b1qh
# hypothesis: the failing build omits a required shared package from the declared cone
git sparse-checkout list
# expected: the active sparse paths expose the intended working set without claiming object completeness
# observation: apps/status is present while packages/shared is absent from the build working set
# changed-case: add packages/shared and repeat both sparse-index status and full-object release verification
# after-ref: main=a11cenn candidate=c0ffeeh
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain the prior sparse specification and support disabling sparse checkout reversibly
# transfer-limit: full release and offline recovery need explicit promised-object and promisor-availability checks
